responses.questionText: Identify one method of lossless compression that can be used to compress the image and describe how the method will reduce the file size.
responses.markScheme:
1 mark for naming method, 1 mark per description to max 2
• Run-length encoding
• Replace sequences of the same colour pixel
• ... with colour code and number of identical pixels
responses.studentAnswer: Run-length Encoding (RLE). The character will be stored once followed by the times it occurs. For example EEEFAAAA will be revorded as E3F1A4. Thus the number of bits is reduced.

responses.questionText: Identify one method of lossless compression that can be used to compress the image and describe how the method will reduce the file size.
responses.markScheme:
1 mark for naming method, 1 mark per description to max 2
• Run-length encoding
• Replace sequences of the same colour pixel
• ... with colour code and number of identical pixels
responses.studentAnswer: Run-length encoding (RLE). It will compress any adjacent strings that have identical character. it will store in two value, the first value represent the type of character, another value is the number of repetition of the character.

responses.questionText: Identify one method of lossless compression that can be used to compress the image and describe how the method will reduce the file size.
responses.markScheme:
1 mark for naming method, 1 mark per description to max 2
• Run-length encoding
• Replace sequences of the same colour pixel
• ... with colour code and number of identical pixels
responses.studentAnswer: Similarity compression. Lossless compression means there will be no data loss before and after, the image will be the same when it's decompressed. Color compression counts the amount of sam colors in the image accuratly, to make sure the image remains the same when decompressing.

responses.questionText: Anya made sure that the image was not subject to any copyright before scanning it. Describe what is meant by copyright.
responses.markScheme:
1 mark per bullet point to max 2
• The formal and legal rights to ownership // intellectual property rights
• Protects against unauthorised reproduction of work
• Provides for legal right of redress
responses.studentAnswer: It is a legal recognition of ownership of a software or something.

responses.questionText: Anya made sure that the image was not subject to any copyright before scanning it. Describe what is meant by copyright.

• The formal and legal rights to ownership // intellectual property rights

responses.studentAnswer: Intellectual property right on the image, program, and many more. Cannot be copied, or action can be taken because it is considered as piracy.

responses.questionText: Anya made sure that the image was not subject to any copyright before scanning it. Describe what is meant by copyright.
responses.markScheme:
1 mark per bullet point to max 2
• The formal and legal rights to ownership // intellectual property rights
• Protects against unauthorised reproduction of work

responses.studentAnswer: Copyright is when the image is authorized or belongs to private, if the image is a copyright, Anya must ask for authorization, if not, Anya's behavior is illegal.

responses.questionText: Melinda and her friends set up a peer-to-peer network between their computers to share data. Describe the key features of a peer-to-peer network.
responses.markScheme:
1 mark per bullet point to max 2
• All computers are of equal status
• Each computer provides access to resources and data // data is distributed
• Computers can communicate and share resources
• Each computer is responsible for its own security
responses.studentAnswer: Instead of having a centralised server, all the nodes are connected to each other through different topologies. In this network a node (Melinda for example) does both sending and receiving of files. If she wants to download a file, she requests from her peers. Distributed parts of the file over the network. So Melinda owns part of the file.

responses.questionText: Melinda and her friends set up a peer-to-peer network between their computers to share data. Describe the key features of a peer-to-peer network.
responses.markScheme:
1 mark per bullet point to max 2
• All computers are of equal status
• Each computer provides access to resources and data // data is distributed
• Computers can communicate and share resources
• Each computer is responsible for its own security
responses.studentAnswer: All members of the network are connected to each other and are considered equals. Each peer has access to anothers peer software and data, allowing peers to share functions and information.

responses.questionText: Melinda and her friends set up a peer-to-peer network between their computers to share data. Describe the key features of a peer-to-peer network.
responses.markScheme:
1 mark per bullet point to max 2
• All computers are of equal status
• Each computer provides access to resources and data // data is distributed
• Computers can communicate and share resources
• Each computer is responsible for its own security
responses.studentAnswer: each computer can both act as a server or client. Also, it is easier for a new computer to join in.

responses.questionText: Describe two drawbacks to Melinda and her friends of using a peer-to-peer network.
responses.markScheme:
1 mark per bullet point to max 2 per drawback
• Reduced security // no central management of security
• … only as secure as the weakest computer on the network
• … each computer is at risk from viruses from other computers
• No central management of backup
• ... if the data from one computer is not backed up it is lost to all of them
• No central management of files/software
• … consistency may be difficult to maintain
• … each computer may have different software from the others
• Individual computers may respond slower
• … because they are being accessed by other computers
responses.studentAnswer:
1. Very easy to get hacked / send malicious code and files. With this network you are placing trust in each 'peer' to send over the correct file. However, someone with malicious intent can send over viruses, trojans, etc. because there is no security check done by the network. 
2. If one node (e.g. Melinda's friend) fails, the other ones might not be able to finish downloading a file. This is because every node contains part of the file. Additionally, if one user has slow (internet) bandwidth they might cause a huge traffic on the network, thus taking a long time to download or bad connection.

responses.questionText: Describe two drawbacks to Melinda and her friends of using a peer-to-peer network.
responses.markScheme:
1 mark per bullet point to max 2 per drawback
• Reduced security // no central management of security
• … only as secure as the weakest computer on the network
• … each computer is at risk from viruses from other computers
• No central management of backup
• ... if the data from one computer is not backed up it is lost to all of them
• No central management of files/software
• … consistency may be difficult to maintain
• … each computer may have different software from the others
• Individual computers may respond slower
• … because they are being accessed by other computers
responses.studentAnswer:
1. A peer-to-peer network becomes harder to manage as the network expands. The more peers that are added, the more complex the system gets. 
2. A peer-to-peer network becomes is less secure and relies on trust. A peer could carry out malicious attacks on other peers, or accidentally introduce malicious software into the network.

responses.questionText: Describe two drawbacks to Melinda and her friends of using a peer-to-peer network.
responses.markScheme:
1 mark per bullet point to max 2 per drawback
• Reduced security // no central management of security
• … only as secure as the weakest computer on the network
• … each computer is at risk from viruses from other computers
• No central management of backup
• ... if the data from one computer is not backed up it is lost to all of them
• No central management of files/software
• … consistency may be difficult to maintain
• … each computer may have different software from the others
• Individual computers may respond slower
• … because they are being accessed by other computers
responses.studentAnswer:
1. It is difficult to manage so many computers in peer to peer network. 
2. It is loss of centralism security.

responses.questionText: Kiara has a washing machine and a refrigerator. She has an embedded system in her washing machine. Describe what is meant by an embedded system, using the washing machine as an example.
responses.markScheme:
1 mark per bullet point to max 2
• Definition: Microprocessor/microcontroller within a larger system // microprocessor/microcontroller that performs one specific task
• Example: e.g. Embedded system in washing machine only controls the programs for the washing cycle // it is part of the washing machine but does not perform any other function within it
responses.studentAnswer: It's a system which performs a specific task, usually simple. For example, washing machine may be turned on or off remotely using an app.

responses.questionText: Kiara has a washing machine and a refrigerator. She has an embedded system in her washing machine. Describe what is meant by an embedded system, using the washing machine as an example.
responses.markScheme:
1 mark per bullet point to max 2
• Definition: Microprocessor/microcontroller within a larger system // microprocessor/microcontroller that performs one specific task
• Example: e.g. Embedded system in washing machine only controls the programs for the washing cycle // it is part of the washing machine but does not perform any other function within it
responses.studentAnswer: An embedded system is that microcomputer and software is embedded in applicants. The embedded system in washing machine is made up of hardware, system software and BIOS. It can enable washing machine to do simple operations like controlling motors, recording time.

responses.questionText: Kiara has a washing machine and a refrigerator. She has an embedded system in her washing machine. Describe what is meant by an embedded system, using the washing machine as an example.
responses.markScheme:
1 mark per bullet point to max 2
• Definition: Microprocessor/microcontroller within a larger system // microprocessor/microcontroller that performs one specific task
• Example: e.g. Embedded system in washing machine only controls the programs for the washing cycle // it is part of the washing machine but does not perform any other function within it
responses.studentAnswer: an embeded system is a small piece of system that can run independently with some simple instruction. For example, the user interface on the washing machine. the embeded system has already been tested and debugged and it only process very limited instruction.
